title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Detective Spence discusses the investigation with Rolly Claude and Inspector Grieve. Rolly notes that the deceased is the only lead to finding André, making the search difficult. Spence suggests that media coverage might prompt André to emerge. After Rolly departs, Spence examines an expensive gold lighter marked with the initials "D.H." and instructs Grieve to trace it through Bond Street shops.

The investigation focuses on a broken watch found at the scene, which points to 9:10. While Grieve suggests this indicates the time of the crime, Spence expresses skepticism, noting that criminals sometimes smash watches to create false alibis. Forensic evidence places the crime between 8 pm and 11 pm. Chief Inspector Graves reports that a gardener, Edwards, saw David Hunter exiting a side door at Flambrook around 7:30 pm, contradicting the belief that Hunter was in London. Spence also considers an unknown lipstick found under a wardrobe in the Stagg hotel, noting the room had been vacant for three weeks.

Spence visits an elegant building near Shepherd's Market to investigate. After speaking with an uniformed staff member, Spence learns that both Mrs. Gordon Claude and David Hunter have been staying there since the previous Saturday. The staff member confirms they were both present the previous night and suggests checking room service records for breakfast deliveries. Spence proceeds to the fourth floor and visits apartment nine, where David Hunter admits him into the residence. There, Spence meets Rosaline Claude, who appears tense.
